data.title: KeBaB: -mer based breaking for finding super-maximal exact matches
data.authors: Nathaniel K Brown, Anas Alhadi, Nour Allam, Nithin Bharathi Kabilan Karpagavalli, Suchith Sridhar Khajjayam, Hamza Wahed, Travis Gagie
data.description: Suppose we have a tool for finding super-maximal exact matches (SMEMs) and we want to use it to find all the long SMEMs between a noisy long read  and a highly repetitive pangenomic reference . Notice that if  and the -mer  does not occur in  then no SMEM of length at least  contains . Therefore, if we have a Bloom filter for the distinct -mers in  and we want to find only SMEMs of length , then when given  we can break it into maximal substrings consisting only of -mers the filter says occur in --which we call pseudo-SMEMs--and search only the ones of length at least . If  is reasonably large and we can choose  well then the Bloom filter should be small (because  is highly repetitive) but the total length of the pseudo-SMEMs we search should also be small (because  is noisy). Now suppose we are interested only in the longest  SMEMs of length at least  between  and . Notice …
